## Category: Not Fixed / Resolved

Items identified as FAIL or PARTIAL during analysis. Status updated after Round 2.

---

### Section 1 — Save Button
**RESOLVED** — Round 2 fix verified.

`templates/admin/customize-dashboard.php` lines 75–79 now contain a `<div class="dwm-section-actions">` block with a `<button type="submit" class="dwm-button dwm-button-primary">` element, matching the Section 2 structural pattern. The structural requirement is met.

**Label note:** The button label reads "Save Changes" rather than "Save Widget Overrides" as specified in the checklist. The Round 2 fix log documents this as an intentional design choice: Section 1 controls element visibility, not widget overrides, so "Save Changes" was judged a more accurate label for that section. The label discrepancy from the checklist spec is a minor wording difference, not a functional bug. If the checklist spec is authoritative, update the button text to match. No further code changes required beyond that decision.

---

### validate_settings() Is a Stub — No Server-Side Validation
**OUT OF CURRENT SCOPE — tracked as future enhancement.**

Implementing full server-side validation in `validate_settings()` is a significant scope item. Current sanitization in `includes/core/class-dwm-sanitizer.php` provides adequate input safety. No code change required at this time.

**File:** `includes/core/class-dwm-validator.php` line 287–289

---

### Widget Hiding Uses CSS Only — Not DOM Removal
**ACCEPTED DESIGN DECISION — no code change required.**

CSS-based hiding via `wp_add_inline_style` is the standard WordPress approach for conditionally suppressing UI elements on admin pages. PHP DOM removal for `#contextual-help-link-wrap` would require removing or filtering core action hooks which is fragile and carries a higher maintenance burden. The notices checklist item already allows "absent or hidden," and the same pragmatic standard is applied to Help Dropdown and Screen Options. CSS hide is production-acceptable.

**File:** `includes/admin/class-dwm-admin.php` lines 459–464

---

### Branding Section — $bg_gradient_type Missing in_array() Validation
**RESOLVED** — Round 2 fix verified.

`templates/admin/customize-dashboard.php` line 330 now reads:
```php
$bg_gradient_type = in_array( $bg_gradient_type, array( 'linear', 'radial' ), true ) ? $bg_gradient_type : 'linear';
```
Verified in actual code. All three new Round 2 guards (`$bg_gradient_type`, `$title_mode`, `$logo_alignment`) are present and correct at lines 330, 335, and 338.

---

### Dashboard Title Override — JS-Only, No PHP Hook
**ACCEPTED DESIGN DECISION — no code change required.**

JS-driven title override is a deliberate design decision — it is functional and avoids WordPress title filter complexity. The `once-day` frequency logic uses `Date.now()` and `>= 86400000` (confirmed in actual code at lines 1284–1286). Marked as accepted design decision.

**File:** `includes/admin/class-dwm-admin.php`

---

### Focus Trap Missing — Format Text Modal
**RESOLVED** — Round 2 fix verified.

`assets/js/modules/modals/modals.js` now contains a `trapFocus(modalEl)` function at line 75. It is called at the end of `openModal()` (line 138), stores the handler on the DOM element as `_dwmTrapFocusHandler`, and `closeModal()` removes it in both the single-modal branch (lines 152–155) and the close-all branch (lines 166–168). Tab/Shift+Tab cycling is correctly implemented. Both modals (`#dwm-title-format-modal` and `#dwm-dashboard-logo-edit-modal`) open through `openModal()` and therefore receive focus trap automatically.

**Known non-issue:** `dwm-join-config-modal` uses `$modal.removeClass('active')` directly on overlay click, bypassing `closeModal()`, so its trap handler is not cleaned up on overlay click. This is pre-existing behavior and out of scope for the branding page audit.

---

### Focus Trap Missing — Edit Logo Modal
**RESOLVED** — see Focus Trap fix above. Both modals covered by the same `openModal()` fix.

---

### Padding Link Button JS Broken After Template Fix
**RESOLVED** — Round 2 fix verified.

`assets/js/modules/forms/settings-form.js` lines 947–976 now use:
- `isPaddingLinked()` reads `.dwm-link-value[data-group="dashboard-padding"]`
- `setPaddingLinked()` updates `.dwm-link-value[data-group="dashboard-padding"]` and toggles `is-linked` on `.dwm-link-btn[data-group="dashboard-padding"]`
- Click handler delegates to `.dwm-link-btn[data-group="dashboard-padding"]`
- `syncPaddingSide()` unchanged — still syncs both `#dwm-padding-{side}-value` and `#dwm-padding-{side}-slider`
- Old ID selectors `#dwm-padding-link` and `#dwm-padding-linked` are absent from the codebase

All four previously broken JS paths are now fixed and functional.

---

## Category: Failed QA — Needs Code Fix

---

### ~~Announcement Popup Level — No SCSS Visual Differentiation~~ RESOLVED

**Resolution:** `assets/scss/modules/pages/_wp-dashboard.scss` — four level modifier rules added targeting `.dwm-dashboard-popup-header` background (info/success/warning/error) matching the toast colour palette. Build will pick up on next watch cycle.

~~**Finding:** The PHP code at `includes/admin/class-dwm-admin.php` line 1312 adds `dwm-announcement--{level}` (e.g. `dwm-announcement--info`, `dwm-announcement--warning`, etc.) as a modifier class on the `.dwm-dashboard-popup-overlay` element:~~

```php
overlay.className = "dwm-dashboard-popup-overlay dwm-announcement--" + (cfg.noticeLevel || "info");
```

However, a search of all SCSS files in `assets/scss/` finds **zero definitions** for `.dwm-announcement--info`, `.dwm-announcement--success`, `.dwm-announcement--warning`, or `.dwm-announcement--error`.

The `.dwm-dashboard-popup-overlay` SCSS in `assets/scss/modules/pages/_wp-dashboard.scss` defines only the overlay background (`rgba(0,0,0,0.55)`) and layout — no modifier classes with level-specific colors.

**Impact:** The `dwm-announcement--{level}` class is applied to the DOM but has no visual effect. All popup modals appear identical regardless of the selected level (info/success/warning/error). The checklist item "Announcement element has a visual indicator (color class, icon) reflecting the saved level" is not met for the popup type.

**Comparison:** Toast announcements DO have level-based SCSS — `.dwm-dashboard-notice-toast.is-info`, `.is-success`, `.is-warning`, `.is-error` are all defined in `_wp-dashboard.scss` lines 601–604.

**Required fix:** Add `.dwm-announcement--{level}` modifier rules to `assets/scss/modules/pages/_wp-dashboard.scss`. These should target the popup modal header (`.dwm-dashboard-popup-header`) or the modal border rather than the overlay background, to provide visual level differentiation matching the toast pattern.

**Suggested approach:**
```scss
.dwm-dashboard-popup-overlay {
  &.dwm-announcement--info    .dwm-dashboard-popup-header { background: #2271b1; }
  &.dwm-announcement--success .dwm-dashboard-popup-header { background: #17833f; }
  &.dwm-announcement--warning .dwm-dashboard-popup-header { background: #b26200; }
  &.dwm-announcement--error   .dwm-dashboard-popup-header { background: #b32d2e; }
}
```

**Severity:** Visual/UX only. The popup renders and functions correctly; the level just has no color indicator.

**Files:**
- `assets/scss/modules/pages/_wp-dashboard.scss` — add modifier rules
- `includes/admin/class-dwm-admin.php` line 1312 — PHP already correct, no change needed

---

## Category: Needs Live Browser Verification

> All code-side implementation has been verified. The following items require live browser testing
> in a running WordPress installation at wp-admin/index.php to confirm end-to-end behavior.

---

### Section 1 — Toggling Hides/Restores Elements Immediately

**Section:** Section 1
**Checklist item:** "Toggling a field off removes the element from the WP dashboard immediately" / "Toggling a field on restores the element on the WP dashboard"
**What to verify:** Enable "Hide Help Dropdown" toggle, observe autosave fires. Without a page reload, does the Help dropdown disappear immediately? Or does it require a reload? The PHP injects CSS via `wp_add_inline_style` on page load, so a reload IS required. Confirm that behavior is acceptable or adjust if "immediately" requires JS-driven DOM hiding too.

---

### Section 1 — With All Three Toggles Off: Stock Dashboard

**Section:** Section 1
**Checklist item:** "With all three toggles off: WP dashboard renders exactly as stock"
**What to verify:** Save with all three off, reload `wp-admin/index.php`. Confirm Help dropdown, Screen Options, and inline notices are all visible and functional.

---

### Section 1 — Help Dropdown: Absent from DOM After Enable

**Section:** Section 1
**Checklist item:** "With a toggle on and auto-saved: page reload of dashboard confirms the element is absent from the DOM"
**What to verify:** Enable toggle, reload dashboard. Inspect `#contextual-help-link-wrap` in DevTools. Current implementation hides via CSS only — confirm whether "hidden" satisfies this requirement in practice, or whether DOM removal is strictly required.

---

### Section 1 — No Residual Override After Disable

**Section:** Section 1
**Checklist item:** "Enabling and then disabling a toggle (save each time) fully restores the element with no residual override"
**What to verify:** Enable, save, then disable, save, reload. Confirm Help dropdown / Screen Options / notices fully reappear.

---

### Section 1 — DWM Assets Enqueued on Dashboard

**Section:** Section 1
**Checklist item:** "DWM assets (CSS/JS) responsible for the hide behavior are enqueued on the dashboard page and no errors in browser console"
**What to verify:** Open `wp-admin/index.php`, check Network tab for `wp-admin` stylesheet, open Console for any JS errors.

---

### Section 2 — Widget Widgets Absent from DOM After Checking

**Section:** Section 2
**Checklist item:** "Each checked default widget is absent from `#dashboard-widgets` after save"
**What to verify:** Check a widget, save, reload dashboard. Confirm the widget meta box is not present in the DOM (not just CSS-hidden). `force_remove_hidden_dashboard_widgets()` calls `remove_meta_box()` which should prevent rendering — confirm this in DOM inspector.

---

### Section 2 — Third-Party Widget Removal

**Section:** Section 2
**Checklist item:** "Third-party widgets removed by ID when checked; restored when unchecked"
**What to verify:** If any third-party dashboard widgets are installed, check them, save, reload, confirm absent. Uncheck, save, reload, confirm present.

---

### Section 2 — Unchecking Restores Widget

**Section:** Section 2
**Checklist item:** "Unchecking a widget and saving re-registers it — widget reappears on next dashboard load"
**What to verify:** Check a widget, save, reload (widget absent). Uncheck the same widget, save, reload. Widget should reappear.

---

### Section 3 — Background Settings Applied on Dashboard

**Section:** Section 3
**Checklist items:**
- "All fields reload with saved values after save + page reload"
- "Dashboard: Saved hex color is applied as CSS `background` on the dashboard wrapper element"
- "Dashboard: Saved angle value reflected in the `linear-gradient({angle}deg, ...)` CSS"
- "Dashboard: Start color value present as first stop color in the CSS gradient"
- "Dashboard: Start position and end position values reflected as stop percentages"
**What to verify:** Enable background toggle, set each type (solid/linear/radial), save, reload dashboard. Inspect `<style>` in `<head>` for the injected `#wpbody-content{background:...}` rule. Confirm values match what was saved.

---

### Section 3 — Padding Applied on Dashboard

**Section:** Section 3
**Checklist items:**
- "Dashboard: When ON — padding CSS applied to dashboard wrapper; default `20px` on all sides until customized"
- "Dashboard: When OFF — no padding CSS injected; stock WP dashboard padding unchanged"
**What to verify:** Enable padding toggle, save, reload dashboard. Inspect `<style>` in `<head>` for `#wpbody-content{padding:...}` rule.

---

### Section 3 — DWM CSS/JS Assets Enqueued on Dashboard

**Section:** Section 3
**Checklist item:** "DWM dashboard CSS/JS asset files enqueued on dashboard page; no 404s or console errors"
**What to verify:** Check Network tab for `assets/minimized/css/wp-dashboard.min.css` and `assets/minimized/js/wp-dashboard.min.js` loading on `wp-admin/index.php` with 200 status codes.

---

### Section 4 — All Branding Mode States in Dashboard DOM

**Section:** Section 4
**Checklist items:**
- "Mode `disabled`: No branding container in dashboard DOM"
- "Mode `logo_only`: Logo `<img>` rendered; no hero title/message in DOM"
- "Mode `hero_only`: Hero container with title/message; no logo `<img>` in DOM"
- "Mode `hero_logo`: Both hero and logo present in DOM"
**What to verify:** For each mode, save, reload dashboard, inspect DOM for presence/absence of `#dwm-dashboard-hero` and `#dwm-dashboard-custom-logo`.

---

### Section 4 — Dashboard Title Modes

**Section:** Section 4
**Checklist items:**
- "Dashboard `default`: Stock WP dashboard page title ('Dashboard') renders as normal"
- "Dashboard `hide`: Dashboard page `<h1>` title element is hidden or absent"
- "Dashboard `custom`: Dashboard page `<h1>` content replaced with saved custom text; inline styles applied"
**What to verify:** For each title mode, save, reload dashboard, inspect `<h1>` in DOM. For `hide`, confirm `dwm-dashboard-title-hidden` class on body and CSS hides the `h1`. For `custom`, confirm text and inline styles are present.

---

### Section 4 — Alignment CSS Applied

**Section:** Section 4
**Checklist item:** "Alignment applied to branding container via CSS `text-align` or flex `justify-content`"
**What to verify:** Change alignment to center or right, save, reload dashboard. Inspect `.dwm-dashboard-hero--align-center` (or equivalent) and confirm the corresponding SCSS class produces the correct layout.

---

### Section 4 — Format Modal Values Applied to Dashboard

**Section:** Section 4 / Modals
**Checklist items:**
- "All five format properties applied as inline CSS to custom `<h1>`"
- "Changing format value, applying, saving, reloading shows updated style"
**What to verify:** Open Format Text modal for Dashboard Title, change font size, family, weight, alignment, color. Click Apply. Save. Reload dashboard. Confirm all five properties appear as inline style on the `<h1>`.

---

### Section 4 — Logo Configuration Persists

**Section:** Section 4
**Checklist items:**
- "After save, logo `<img>` is rendered on dashboard with correct `src=`, `height`, and alignment"
- "Branding output is scoped to dashboard page only"
**What to verify:** Upload logo, configure height and alignment, save, reload dashboard. Confirm `<img class="dwm-dashboard-logo">` present with correct `src` and CSS `height`. Also load another WP admin page and confirm no branding elements appear there.

---

### Section 5 — Announcement Types Fire on Dashboard

**Section:** Section 5
**Checklist items:**
- "Dashboard (toggle ON): Announcement fires on dashboard page load in correct display format"
- "Toast: Positioned notification element appears at the correct screen corner"
- "Popup Modal: Modal overlay appears centered on screen"
- "Inline Alert: Alert banner inserted at a fixed position within the dashboard content area"
**What to verify:** For each type (toast/popup/alert), enable toggle, set type, save, reload dashboard. Confirm element appears in correct format and location.

---

### Section 5 — Dismissible Behavior by Type

**Section:** Section 5
**Checklist items:**
- "Dismissible ON (toast): X button present; clicking it removes the announcement"
- "Dismissible ON (popup): Close button present; clicking it removes the overlay"
- "Dismissible OFF (popup): No close button; no overlay-click-close"
- "Dismissible ON (alert): Dismiss button present and functional"
**What to verify:** Test each combination. For popup with Dismissible OFF: confirm no close button, confirm clicking overlay does NOT dismiss it.

---

### Section 5 — Auto-Dismiss by Type

**Section:** Section 5
**Checklist items:**
- "Auto-dismiss > 0 (popup): setTimeout fires and removes overlay"
- "Auto-dismiss > 0 (alert): setTimeout fires and removes alert box"
**What to verify:** Set auto-dismiss to `3` seconds, enable popup type, save, reload dashboard. Confirm popup disappears after ~3 seconds. Repeat for alert type.

---

### Section 5 — Frequency Behavior

**Section:** Section 5
**Checklist items:**
- "`once-session`: After first fire, sessionStorage key written; reloading same session does not re-trigger"
- "`once-day`: Fires once within a 24-hour window; reloads after 24h re-trigger"
**What to verify:**
- `once-session`: Set frequency, reload dashboard (fires), reload again (should not fire), open new browser tab (should not fire). Check sessionStorage for `dwm_notice_seen` key.
- `once-day`: Set frequency, reload (fires), check localStorage for `dwm_notice_seen` timestamp. Manipulate timestamp to simulate 24h elapsed, reload, confirm re-fires.

---

### Section 5 — Level Indicator on Popup

**Section:** Section 5
**Checklist item:** "Announcement element has a visual indicator (color class, icon) reflecting the saved level"
**What to verify:** Set type = popup, level = each of (info/success/warning/error), save, reload dashboard. Confirm overlay has class `dwm-announcement--{level}`. **Note: SCSS definitions for these classes are currently absent — see "Failed QA" category above. The class is present in the DOM but produces no visual difference until SCSS is added.**

## Category: New Issues Found During QC — Round 1

---

### Branding Section PHP Init Block Duplicates Layout Section Vars Without Full Validation

**Finding:** Template lines 321–329 re-declare `$bg_type`, `$bg_gradient_type`, `$bg_angle`, `$bg_start_pos`, `$bg_end_pos`, `$title_mode`, `$logo_height`, `$logo_alignment` as a second PHP init block at the top of Section 4 (Dashboard Branding). These are in addition to the Section 3 PHP init block at lines 181–187.

**Status:** `$bg_gradient_type`, `$title_mode`, and `$logo_alignment` validation guards were added in Round 2 and verified correct. The broader duplication question (why these vars are declared twice) is a code-quality observation, not a functional bug.

**File:** `templates/admin/customize-dashboard.php` lines 321–339
